As he moves energetically, high-fiving and shaking hands, voices shout encouraging words like "Alright, hey!", "Come on!", "Yeah!", and "Woo!", all set against the continuing upbeat music. The hallway features framed photos and a neon sign for 'The Late Late Show'. He then turns back, descending the stairs, still visibly upset, as the music continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The Man in Suit, identified as James, stands before a black, industrial-style stage door labeled 'ST-2,' secured with a keypad lock. The door is adorned with multiple signs: a red-bordered notice about turning off cell phones, a 'DO NOT ENTER' sign, and a 'RECORDING IN PROGRESS' sign indicating 4:30-7:00 PM daily. James repeatedly attempts to open the door, first by trying the handle, then by pressing the keypad, his expressions shifting from focused determination to growing frustration. He asks, "What's the problem?" and a calm, synthesized voice responds, "Door's locked." James, exasperated, questions, "No, but why? Why is the door locked?" The voice explains, "Issue with the passcode." James insists he can do it, asking for the passcode. The voice provides "4 5 6 5," which James tries on the keypad, but the door remains locked, prompting him to declare, "No, it's not." The voice then suggests "4 5 6 6," which James also tries, again failing and stating, "No, it's not that, that's not it." As the voice offers "4 5 6 7," James, gesturing with his hands and placing them on his hips, asks incredulously, "Are you just making up numbers?" to which the voice calmly replies, "Yes." His frustration peaks as he exclaims, "Oh my god," and the voice, addressing him as "James," insists, "we have to start the show." James, looking back as if calling for help or expressing disbelief, protests, "No, I'm not... I'm starting my final show in a... in a stairwell." Despite his protests and declaration of "I don't care," the voice firmly reiterates, "The network insists. Show has to start." James, unable to gain entry, expresses his disbelief, "Oh my god, I can't believe this," as the door remains firmly locked throughout, underscoring the barrier in the stark, white-walled corridor.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with James, the host of "The Late Late Show," in a dark suit and red tie, visibly frustrated as he repeatedly attempts to open a locked stage door labeled 'ST-2'. He tries the handle, presses the keypad, and gestures emphatically, his body language shifting from determined to exasperated. He welcomes the audience to "The Late Late Show," calling them a "hard crowd," and thanks his family for coming out. His agitation grows as he declares, "I'm not doing this, it's ridiculous," before introducing the show's guest, Will Ferrell, the star of "Elf," "Step Brothers," and the "Barbie movie." Loud thudding sounds are heard as he continues to struggle with the door, which features a red 'DO NOT ENTER' sign and a 'RECORDING IN PROGRESS' notice. James expresses his inability to see Will because he lacks a screen for the "dressing room check-in." A calm, off-screen voice states "Standby," followed by another thud, then informs James, "Will is at the window." James responds with a confused "What?" as the voice repeats the information. Suddenly, an urgent voice, presumably Will Ferrell, calls out "James!" multiple times from behind the door, adding "Up here!" James recognizes "Will!" and shouts "Hey!" and "I can't hear you!" through the door, muttering in confusion as another thud sounds. Displaying his annoyance, he places his hands on his hips, shakes his head, points at the unyielding barrier, leans against the door, and peers through its small window before turning away in frustration. He then walks down the short industrial-style staircase, glancing back at the door, and continues through a narrow corridor with purple lighting and framed photos, eventually reaching a hallway with a neon sign reading 'The Late Late Show,' underscoring the television studio setting and his urgent, yet thwarted, need to enter the restricted area.

He passionately declares, "This show is everything. It is everything I ever wanted it to be," his voice filled with emotion as the audience responds with enthusiastic clapping and cheering. The backdrop features a stylized nighttime cityscape with illuminated buildings and a purple-hued sky, subtly displaying the show’s logo. On his desk, a black mug with the show’s branding and a small bell are visible. The camera briefly cuts to the audience, showing a man in a brown jacket clapping and smiling, seated at a separate desk with a microphone and a white mug labeled 'KARMEI'. Returning to Corden, he continues his energetic delivery, occasionally placing a hand on his chest or gesturing outward, humbly stating, "I am just one part of a big team who make this show, this incredible family here, who have made The Late Late Show." He reflects on the show's history, mentioning, "they started this show on day one and here we are 1200 shows later," before expressing profound gratitude: "I would like to take this moment to thank each and every one of you who works on this show from the bottom of my heart for the love and the dedication that you have given to making this show what it is today." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a dimly lit stage, where James Corden, dressed in a dark blue suit, white shirt, and red tie, is seated at a glossy black grand piano, illuminated by dramatic, warm spotlights cutting through a hazy atmosphere. He sings passionately into a microphone, his facial expressions emotive, eyes closed at times, conveying deep feeling as he delivers poignant lyrics about the show's end: "The show fast approaches. I gaze at the studio floor. We filled it with thousands of moments of joy. And now there won't be any more." The camera slowly zooms in, capturing his intensity as he continues, "Yet I know as I look in the camera for the very last time as your host, that each of those thousands of moments of joy is the one I'll remember the most." Behind him, a choir of performers, mostly women with long hair, stands in silhouette, their harmonies blending with Corden's melancholic singing and the piano's melody. The stage lighting fixtures cast golden beams, enhancing the moody, theatrical atmosphere. Corden reflects, "And part of me thinks why not stay here forever, but deep in my heart I just know... No more shows to be showing. It's time I was going. It's time. Thanks for watching. That's our show." The audience, visible as attentive silhouettes, erupts in applause as he finishes, marking a powerful and emotional conclusion.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with James Corden, in a dark suit and red tie, seated at a glossy black grand piano on a dimly lit, smoke-hazed stage, singing an emotional ballad into a microphone with expressive gestures under warm, golden spotlights. As the song concludes, he addresses the enthusiastically clapping and cheering audience, stating, "everybody, I will miss you. Goodnight." He then stands and walks down the center aisle, waving to the standing crowd, while a powerful and emotional orchestral instrumental piece continues to play. Reaching the stage edge, he offers a final wave before exiting through a curtain beneath 'THE LATE LATE SHOW' marquee, as the audience's applause and cheers persist. The scene transitions to end credits, including 'EXECUTIVE PRODUCER BRIAN TILLY' and 'EXECUTIVE PRODUCER JAMES CORDEN,' rolling over the audience, with the music gradually fading. The segment concludes with the 'fulwell73 PRODUCTIONS' and 'CBS BROADCASTING INC.' logos appearing against a dark, blurred background with lens flares.

As he finishes his performance, he stands and walks toward the audience, who are standing and clapping enthusiastically. The camera follows him as he moves down the center aisle, waving to the crowd.
